creator: rkoberman@gmail.com
text:
While there is a bug as the system should not panic when there is a network problem, I am unable t collect further information as the problem was occurring while I was on vacation at a resort. Now that I am home, the interface is operating normally. I may be able to add some details that might help, though.

The network was unstable and the initial problem occurred when the network was dropping a lot of packets and going down for periods of several seconds. In this case, network failed when the system was idle and had been down for an unknown, but extended time. The network showed the association lost. I tried to restart the wpa_supplicant. The association was restored, but no address assigned, so I restarted the dhclient. The network remained unreachable for no obvious reason, so I did an netif restart. The system panicked.

When I tried rebooting he system, it crashed when the interface started. Even after a power cycle, the same crashed occurred. I ws unable to get past the attempt to start the wlan.

I plugged in my old urtwn fob and it came up. The network remained unstable, but I was able to restart and it worked. I then switched back to the iwlwifi interface and the startup crashes resumed. As the crash happened as soon as the network was started, it is not possible that the interface associated.

When I returned home, it just worked immediately. I only got a single dump... when the system failed when it had been running and lost connectivity. I am unsure why there were no others. I fear this will need to be closed as "Unable to reproduce". Sorry.
